title: Plant Growth and Photosynthesis
intro_hook: Every breath you take traces back to a plant. How does a plant make the oxygen you are breathing right now?
big_idea: Photosynthesis: 6CO2 + 6H2O + light -> C6H12O6 + 6O2. Plants are solar-powered chemical factories that create food AND oxygen from sunlight, water, and air.

cause_process_result.cause: Sunlight hits chlorophyll in leaf cells
cause_process_result.process: Light energy splits water; CO2 absorbed from air; glucose synthesized in Calvin cycle
cause_process_result.result: Glucose (plant food) produced; O2 released as byproduct

flow: Sunlight hits chlorophyll in leaf cells
Light energy splits water; CO2 absorbed from air; glucose synthesized in Calvin cycle
Glucose (plant food) produced; O2 released as byproduct
whiteboard: Cause: Sunlight hits chlorophyll in leaf cells
-> Light energy splits water; CO2 absorbed from air; glucose synthesized in Calvin cycle
Result: Glucose (plant food) produced; O2 released as byproduct
Key concepts: Photosynthesis, Chlorophyll, Growth Stages, Ecosystem Role
indian_analogies: Large mature tree produces ~100kg O2 per year — enough for 2 people
Autumn colors: chlorophyll breaks down revealing hidden yellow/orange carotenoids

misconceptions.belief: Plants get their food from soil
misconceptions.probe: If plants eat soil, why doesn't the pot of soil disappear over time?
misconceptions.repair: Plants make their own food through photosynthesis using CO2 from AIR and water. Soil provides minerals — not the main carbon for glucose.

misconceptions.belief: Greener plants always produce more oxygen
misconceptions.probe: Some plants are purple or red but still photosynthesize. Why?
misconceptions.repair: It is chlorophyll content that drives photosynthesis — not greenness per se. Red/purple plants have other pigments but still contain chlorophyll.

question_flow.q: Every breath you take traces back to a plant. How does a plant make the oxygen you are breathing right now?
What is the most surprising thing about this concept?
question_flow.a: Yeh sabse adbhut baat hai ki paudhe suraj ki roshni, hawa aur paani se apna bhojan bana sakte hain, jise hum photosynthesis kehte hain. Is prakriya mein paudhe oxygen bhi utpann karte hain jo humare liye bahut mahatvapoorn hai. Yeh prakriya humare jivan ke liye atyant aavash

question_flow.q: Trace through: Sunlight hits chlorophyll in leaf cells — what happens step by step?
Ab tu mujhe bata — apne words mein. Koi analogy use karo.
If plants eat soil, why doesn't the pot of soil disappear over time?
Some plants are purple or red but still photosynthesize. Why?

practice_problems.q: Explain in your own words: Photosynthesis equation: 6CO2 + 6H2O + light -> C6H12O6 + 6O2
Explain in your own words: Ocean phytoplankton produce 50-80% of Earth's oxygen
practice_problems.answer: Ocean phytoplankton produce 50-80% of Earth's oxygen

practice_problems.q: Explain in your own words: Photosynthesis efficiency only 3-6% — yet powers all life
practice_problems.answer: Photosynthesis efficiency only 3-6% — yet powers all life

Large mature tree produces ~100kg O2 per year — enough for 2 people

indian_legacy.connection: In Hindu mythology, the Ashoka tree is associated with the story of Janaka, a king who attained spiritual enlightenment under its shade. The unique properties of the Ashoka tree, which can thrive in a variety of conditions and is known for its ability to purify the air, can be used to illustrate the concept of photosynthesis and how plants like the Ashoka tree use sunlight, water, and air to produce food and oxygen.
indian_legacy.story_hook: Imagine you are a young prince like Janaka, sitting under the shade of a majestic Ashoka tree, wondering how it can grow so tall and strong with just sunlight, water, and air. As you discover the magic of photosynthesis, you realize that the Ashoka tree is not just a symbol of wisdom, but also a powerful example of how plants are the unsung heroes of our planet, producing oxygen and food for all living beings.
